consensus: Stuck detection is purely time-based (120s claim-file mtime); no heartbeat or output-progress check
Recovery (orphan requeue) only triggers when ALL agents are dead AND claim stale >180s; single-dead-worker case is unrecovered
No automatic restart mechanism exists anywhere in the codebase
Zero test coverage for any recovery/resilience scenario
README overstates recovery capability (claims PID-based detection; reality is tmux pane status)
Depth Guard and partial-status enforcement are prompt-convention-only, not platform-enforced
key_gaps: Single-dead-worker orphan: task stays claimed forever if other agents still alive
No trap-based cleanup on launcher abnormal exit
Shared working directory across agents creates blast radius risk
No fleet-level exit code or programmatic success validation
Stuck detection cannot distinguish slow-but-working from truly hung
